facts.text: Prague’s name comes from the old Czech word pražiti, meaning “to roast,” hinting at the hot, arid spot where the castle first rose.
Vyšehrad, a prominent Prague fort founded in the 10th century, stands about 70 years after the original Prague Castle and overlooks the river.
Charles University, founded in 1347 by King Charles IV, is the oldest university in Central Europe and a historic center of learning.
Since 1992 the historic centre of Prague has been a UNESCO World Heritage Site, preserving its medieval streets and architectural treasures.
